channel.title: CyberKareem security writing
channel.description: Security research, engineering notes, certification reviews, and authorized-lab walkthroughs by Abdullah Kareem.

item.title: CVE-2026-55675: Fork PR Code Ran in a Privileged GitHub Actions Job
item.description: Untrusted pull-request code reached a CI job holding deployment secrets. Separating build from deploy removed that path.

item.title: CVE-2026-55890: Grav's style() Method Bypassed the Earlier Fix
item.description: Grav blocked style through attribute(), but the sibling style() action still accepted editor-controlled CSS.

item.title: CVE-2026-59185: Missing Installation Ownership Check Allowed Cross-Tenant Access
item.description: The backend accepted a GitHub App installation ID without verifying that the authorizing GitHub user could access that installation.

item.title: CVE-2026-59889: @JsonUnwrapped Replay Skipped an @JsonView Check
item.description: Jackson enforced @JsonView for creator properties but skipped the check when @JsonUnwrapped replayed field- and setter-backed properties.

item.title: CVE-2026-59979: Missing Tenant Checks in Yosemite-Crew Routes
item.description: Several read, update, and delete routes either lacked authentication or stopped at identity checks before globally keyed lookups.

item.title: CVE-2026-61448: Malformed Content-Type Led to Stored XSS in Parse Server
item.description: Parse Server accepted malformed media types for unknown extensions, letting some storage adapters serve uploads that browsers could sniff as HTML.

item.title: CVE-2026-61706: Pull Request Title Injection in Infracost Actions
item.description: A pull request title was inserted directly into a Bash run block, allowing GitHub Actions expression expansion to turn untrusted text into runner commands.

item.title: CVE-2026-61826: Bazarr's Plex OAuth API Lacked Authentication
item.description: Seventeen Plex API methods lacked Bazarr's normal API-key gate, exposing persistent configuration changes and, on configured instances, token-bearing server requests.

item.title: Building seg-test: A PCI DSS Segmentation Evidence Runner
item.description: seg-test runs approved checks from defined source VLANs, preserves raw Nmap output, and packages evidence without making compliance decisions.

item.title: Secure Automated Purge: Building a Bootable Disk-Sanitization Utility
item.description: An archived engineering account of a bootable disk-sanitization prototype that requires device-specific validation and review against NIST SP 800-88 Rev.2 before operational use.

item.title: What Ninety Days of Stoic Practice Changed for Me
item.description: A personal account of using Stoic practices to manage anxiety, build consistency, and carry those habits into security work.

item.title: BitLocker Cryptographic Erase Utility: Design, Limits, and Validation
item.description: A reviewed account of a BitLocker-based cryptographic-erase prototype, its operating assumptions, and the validation required before real-world use.

item.title: Microsoft Teams Classic vs. New Teams: A 2023 Security Comparison
item.description: A point-in-time comparison of Microsoft Teams Classic and New Teams, preserved as historical product and security context rather than current guidance.

item.title: Offensive Security Wireless Professional (OSWP) Certification Review
item.description: A retrospective OSWP review covering the PEN-210 learning path, practical wireless lab work, exam preparation, and lessons for future candidates.

item.title: What I Learned from Ninety Days of Personal Journaling
item.description: A personal reflection on ninety days of journaling, the routines that made it sustainable, and the lessons carried into professional practice.

item.title: eCPPTv2 Certification Review
item.description: A retrospective eCPPTv2 review covering preparation, the practical assessment, reporting, and lessons from earning the certification in 2022.

item.title: Installing Metasploit Framework on Ubuntu: Historical Guide
item.description: A preserved 2022 installation note for Metasploit Framework on Ubuntu. Commands and repository setup may have changed and require current upstream verification.
